title: Watch Me Build an AI Resume Analysis System in 28 minutes (no code)

extracted.hook: What we're looking at today is the smart AI ré collection system that not only analyzes the candidate for strengths and weaknesses, but it will also look at the job description that they're applying for and it will tell you what's the risk here, what's the reward, what's the overall fit, and then it will give you some justification for it.
extracted.credential_drop: "I'm going to do today is build this right in front of you guys live. I'm going to tell you guys my thought process as I'm building it and by the end you'll have it all set up for yourself." (late)

accelerate: discussing the thought process and steps involved in building the system
slow: explaining the wireframe and decision-making process for the workflow

extracted.cta: "if you enjoyed, if you learned something new, please give it a like"
transitions: "So, what I'm going to do first before we start building is tell you guys about how I approach anything that I want to automate and how I think about it. So this is a really important mindset shift to get in the habit of which is basically listing out the steps that you would do manually if you wanted to complete this process."

"And so, I'm going to do today is build this right in front of you guys live. I'm going to tell you guys my thought process as I'm building it and by the end you'll have it all set up for yourself. Okay"
"Okay, so when it comes to wireframing, it doesn't have to be perfect, but you want to have a general idea of what you're going to build so that you get into edit end and you can just build it."

"And then in this case, we can see that there's a decision to be made right here, which is basically what type of file is the resume. But we can make this decision without using any AI. So we don't need an agent and we keep the workflow really simple. So let's real quick go through this wireframe and understand at a high level how the system works."

"Okay, so when it comes to wireframing, it doesn't have to be perfect, but you want to have a general idea of what you're going to build so that you get into edit end and you can just build it. You're going to save yourself a lot of time. Imagine if you open up a Lego box for a tractor and you started trying to build the tractor without looking at the instruction manual. And now we're going to write all the data back to our Google sheet
